items.q: Which chamber pumps oxygenated blood to the whole body?
items.why: The left ventricle has the thickest wall to force blood around the whole systemic circulation.

items.q: What is the job of the heart valves?
items.correct: To prevent the backflow of blood, keeping it flowing one way.
items.random_wrong: To make the heartbeat sound for no reason.
items.why: Valves ensure one-way flow; the heart muscle, not the valves, does the pumping.

items.q: How many chambers does the human heart have?
items.correct: Four - two atria and two ventricles.
items.why: The mammalian heart has four chambers, separating oxygenated and deoxygenated blood.

items.q: Which chamber pumps blood to the entire body?
items.why: Correct answer: Left ventricle. “Right atrium” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which vessel type has valves to prevent backflow?
items.why: Correct answer: Veins. “Arteries” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many chambers are in the human heart?
items.why: Exact answer: 4. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
